meta.sprintTitle: Admin Console v1 - Admin Auth + RBAC
meta.goal: Introduce a secure Admin Console authentication and role-based access control foundation so authorised internal users can access operational tools without exposing customer data or privileged actions to normal users.

basedOnPriorSprints: Error handling and graceful recovery planning
whyNow: Public launch and alpha operations need internal visibility and controlled access
Support, export troubleshooting, billing review, and moderation will require privileged screens
Admin must be isolated from normal user auth and protected with auditable permissions

nonGoals: Full admin dashboard implementation for all modules
Refund workflows or direct payment operations
Public backoffice APIs for third parties
dependencies: Audit log persistence table or equivalent

security: 0 normal authenticated users can access admin routes
0 unauthorised API calls to admin endpoints succeed
All admin actions are attributable to an admin identity
operational: Internal admins can log in and reach the admin console
Admin roles restrict visibility and actions correctly
Audit logs are generated for auth and privileged actions
quality: Permission guard coverage for 100% of admin routes and APIs
Role checks are centralised, not duplicated ad hoc in UI
Admin auth and RBAC flows are covered by automated tests

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Full system operator with access to all admin modules and privileged actions.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Can inspect users, projects, and export states to resolve support issues, but cannot change system-wide security settings.

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: Can manage templates, prompts, public content, and moderate content, but cannot access sensitive billing or security controls.

personas.name: Normal App User
personas.description: Regular Memorioso customer. Must never gain admin access.

inScope: Dedicated admin route group and layout
Admin user record or role mapping
Audit logging for admin login and privileged actions
Seed/bootstrap flow for initial super admin
Test coverage for auth and permission boundaries
outOfScope: Granular field-level masking beyond defined scope

architecture.recommendedApproach: Keep admin access within the same application stack but fully isolated via dedicated route namespace, dedicated permission checks, and server-side enforcement. Use role-to-permission mapping with a small fixed role set for v1.

authStrategy.preferred: Reuse existing authentication/session provider, then layer an admin eligibility check on top.
authStrategy.fallback: Separate admin sign-in page using same auth backend but only allowing users flagged as admin.
rules: Authentication alone is not sufficient; admin role must also be present
All admin route and API checks must be enforced server-side
Client-side checks are UX only and not security boundaries

rbacModel.type: Role-based access control with permission strings
principles: Single source of truth for permissions

tables.name: admin_users
tables.purpose: Maps a primary user account to admin eligibility and role.

fields.name: user_id
fields.notes: FK to app user/auth user, unique

tables.name: admin_audit_logs
tables.purpose: Stores security and privileged admin events.

fields.name: admin_user_id
fields.notes: Null allowed for denied attempts if needed

optionalFutureTables.name: admin_role_permissions
optionalFutureTables.whenNeeded: Only if roles become configurable rather than code-defined.

userStories.title: Admin can sign in to admin console
userStories.iWant: to access a protected admin area
userStories.soThat: I can perform internal operational tasks securely

userStories.title: Normal users are blocked from admin area
userStories.iWant: admin routes to be inaccessible to me
userStories.soThat: privileged data and controls remain protected

userStories.title: Role-based module visibility
userStories.iWant: to see only the modules I am permitted to use

userStories.title: Server-side permission enforcement
userStories.iWant: API and route authorization enforced on the server
userStories.soThat: client-side bypasses cannot expose privileged functionality

userStories.title: Audit privileged access
userStories.iWant: admin auth and sensitive actions logged
userStories.soThat: I can trace who did what and when

userStories.title: Bootstrap initial super admin
userStories.iWant: a safe way to create the first super admin
userStories.soThat: the admin console can be used without manual DB confusion

functionalRequirements.title: Dedicated admin entrypoint
requirements: Add an /admin route group with isolated layout and navigation
Unauthenticated visitors attempting /admin are redirected to sign-in
Authenticated non-admin users attempting /admin receive access denied or safe redirect

functionalRequirements.title: Admin eligibility lookup
requirements: After authentication, system checks whether the user exists in admin_users with active status
Disabled admins cannot access admin area even if they remain normally authenticated
Admin eligibility check must be available to both route guards and APIs

functionalRequirements.title: Role resolution and permission mapping
requirements: System resolves current admin role for each request/session
Permissions are computed from a central role-permission map
Unknown or missing roles must fail closed

functionalRequirements.title: Admin navigation rendering
requirements: Admin sidebar/top nav only shows modules permitted for current admin
Hidden nav items are not sufficient security; matching route guards are still required
Optional disabled-state messaging may be shown for discoverability only if safe

functionalRequirements.title: Admin API authorization
requirements: All admin APIs require authenticated admin context
Each endpoint checks required permission string before executing action
Forbidden responses use consistent error shape

functionalRequirements.title: Admin session UX
requirements: Admin users can sign out cleanly
Admin layout shows current admin identity and role
Expired sessions should redirect to sign-in without exposing sensitive state

functionalRequirements.title: Audit logging
requirements: Log admin login success
Log denied admin access attempts where feasible
Log admin role changes
Log access to sensitive modules and privileged actions where feasible in v1
Audit logs must include timestamp and actor identity

functionalRequirements.title: Initial admin bootstrap
requirements: Provide a secure bootstrap method to create the first super_admin
Bootstrap path must not remain publicly callable after setup
Bootstrap action must be documented for local, staging, and production use

functionalRequirements.title: Admin role management v1
requirements: Only super_admin can grant, change, disable, or reactivate admin roles
Support_admin and content_admin cannot manage other admins
System prevents removal of the last active super_admin without explicit safe-guard logic

functionalRequirements.title: Access denied handling
requirements: Show clear but non-revealing access denied page/state for forbidden modules
Do not leak existence of protected records or actions in error messages
Forbidden and unauthenticated states must be distinguishable internally for logging

technicalTasks.title: Define admin domain model
technicalTasks.description: Create admin_users and admin_audit_logs schema, enums, indexes, and migrations.

technicalTasks.title: Create central RBAC module
technicalTasks.description: Implement role enum, permission constants, role-to-permission map, and helper functions such as hasPermission and requirePermission.

technicalTasks.title: Implement admin auth guard
technicalTasks.description: Build server-side middleware/guard for /admin routes and admin APIs using current auth/session provider.

technicalTasks.title: Build admin shell
technicalTasks.description: Add /admin layout, placeholder dashboard page, role-aware navigation, current admin identity chip, and sign-out.

technicalTasks.title: Add admin bootstrap flow
technicalTasks.description: Provide documented secure method to seed first super admin via script, env-protected endpoint, or migration-time tool.

technicalTasks.title: Add admin role management API surface
technicalTasks.description: Create minimal internal APIs for super_admin to list admins, assign role, disable admin, reactivate admin.

technicalTasks.title: Audit logging integration
technicalTasks.description: Write audit events for sign-in success/failure and admin role mutations.

technicalTasks.title: Access denied and forbidden UX
technicalTasks.description: Add reusable forbidden state component/page for admin module access failures.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and route protection tests.

apiSurface.note: Naming is indicative; adapt to existing app conventions.

endpoints.purpose: Returns current admin identity, role, and effective permissions.
Grant admin role to an existing app user.
Change role, disable, or reactivate admin.

error.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.

pages.title: Admin Dashboard
pages.purpose: Placeholder landing page confirming admin access and surfacing role-aware modules.

pages.title: Admin Users
pages.purpose: Minimal internal page for viewing and managing admin roles.

uxRules: Admin UI should clearly indicate the operator is in Admin Console
Use a visually distinct shell from customer-facing app
Avoid clutter; only show modules relevant to role
Do not expose internal IDs or sensitive raw metadata unless necessary
Use confirmation for destructive role actions like disable admin

securityRequirements: All admin authorization decisions must happen server-side
No admin data should be returned from public/user APIs
Admin routes must not be indexable by search engines
Sensitive admin pages should set noindex and be excluded from sitemap
Role changes must be audit logged
Disabled admins lose admin access immediately on next authorization check
The system must not trust role information coming only from client state
Prevent privilege escalation by disallowing self-promotion from non-super-admin roles
Prevent deletion/disable of the final active super_admin unless a safe replacement exists
Avoid leaking whether a target user exists when unauthorised

metrics: count of denied admin access attempts
count of active admins by role
loggingNotes: Do not log secrets or tokens
Mask or minimise personal data in application logs
Audit logs are for traceability; app logs are for debugging

acceptanceCriteria.then: they are denied access and cannot view admin content
they can access the admin console and only see modules allowed for support_admin

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to call a billing admin API
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request is rejected with a forbidden response

acceptanceCriteria.when: they grant admin access to an existing user
acceptanceCriteria.then: the new admin record is created and the action is audit logged

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access the admin console
acceptanceCriteria.then: they are denied even if their base user session remains valid

acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin action that requires a specific permission
acceptanceCriteria.when: the current admin lacks that permission
acceptanceCriteria.then: both the UI path and the API path are blocked

acceptanceCriteria.given: the first production deployment of admin console
acceptanceCriteria.then: exactly one initial super_admin can be created securely

acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin login or role mutation
acceptanceCriteria.then: an audit log entry exists with actor, event type, and timestamp

unitTests: hasPermission returns false for unknown roles
integrationTests: admin guard blocks non-admin user on protected route
admin API returns 403 for insufficient role
e2eTests: super_admin login and access admin dashboard
audit log created on role change
negativeTests: forged client role does not bypass server checks
attempt to disable last active super_admin is rejected

risks.risk: Admin protection only implemented in UI, not server
risks.mitigation: Mandatory server-side route/API guard and authz tests

risks.risk: Ad hoc permission checks become inconsistent
risks.mitigation: Centralise permission constants and helper APIs

risks.risk: Bootstrap flow leaves a permanent backdoor
risks.mitigation: One-time or env-protected bootstrap with documented shutdown/removal
Mandatory audit log writes for auth and role changes

futureSprintHooks: Sprint 49: Feedback, moderation, and support inbox
Sprint 50: Feature flags and operational controls

definitionOfDone: Admin schema migrated in all environments
At least one super_admin can be provisioned safely
Server-side RBAC guards protect admin routes and APIs
Audit logging is active for auth and role changes
Automated tests cover positive and negative access paths
Ops/developer documentation for bootstrap and role management is written
